risk_description: A significant risk: a compatibility issue between the staff training programme and the existing point-of-sale hardware, which may require escalation to senior management to resolve issues with the staff training programme.

A moderate risk: a performance bottleneck in the mobile money integration under the low-bandwidth conditions typical in Livingstone, which could delay the mobile money integration by one to two weeks if not addressed promptly.

A noticeable risk: a budget overrun caused by unforeseen consulting fees on the new inventory management system, which could delay the new inventory management system by one to two weeks if not addressed promptly.

A noticeable risk: a budget overrun caused by unforeseen consulting fees on the solar power installation, which has raised concerns among the project team about the quality of the solar power installation.

A moderate risk: broader economic instability affecting business confidence in the financial reporting system, which could delay the financial reporting system by one to two weeks if not addressed promptly.
A severe risk: a licensing conflict with third-party software used in the customer database migration, which could halt the customer database migration entirely until resolved.

A significant risk: a logistics disruption during the rainy season affecting deliveries for the mobile money integration, which may require escalation to senior management to resolve issues with the mobile money integration.

A growing risk: a licensing conflict with third-party software used in the e-commerce platform rollout, which could delay the e-commerce platform rollout by one to two weeks if not addressed promptly.
A significant risk: high staff turnover during the implementation of the point-of-sale upgrade, which may require escalation to senior management to resolve issues with the point-of-sale upgrade.

A noticeable risk: frequent change requests from stakeholders affecting the customer database migration, which could delay the customer database migration by one to two weeks if not addressed promptly.
A severe risk: rainy-season disruptions to site logistics for the warehouse expansion project in Kabwe, which may result in the SME abandoning the warehouse expansion project altogether.
A severe risk: a software bug discovered in the core module of the mobile money integration, which may result in the SME abandoning the mobile money integration altogether.
A potentially catastrophic risk: a supplier price increase affecting the procurement plan for the branch network expansion, which could halt the branch network expansion entirely until resolved.
A noticeable risk: rainy-season disruptions to site logistics for the branch network expansion in Lusaka, which could delay the branch network expansion by one to two weeks if not addressed promptly.
A slight risk: additional feature requests expanding the original scope of the mobile money integration, which was identified early and resolved with minimal effort.

A significant risk: a misalignment between business requirements and the technical design of the warehouse expansion project, which may require escalation to senior management to resolve issues with the warehouse expansion project.

A noticeable risk: frequent ZESCO load shedding affecting day-to-day operations during the solar power installation, which has raised concerns among the project team about the quality of the solar power installation.

A moderate risk: unclear requirements from management regarding the scope of the e-commerce platform rollout, which is likely to require additional resources to keep the e-commerce platform rollout on track.
A slight risk: rescheduled staff training sessions delaying the rollout of the staff training programme, which was identified early and resolved with minimal effort.

A major risk: reliance on part-time staff to support the e-commerce platform rollout after hours, which may require escalation to senior management to resolve issues with the e-commerce platform rollout.
A minor risk: a logistics disruption during the rainy season affecting deliveries for the mobile money integration, which was identified early and resolved with minimal effort.

A minor risk: reliance on part-time staff to support the warehouse expansion project after hours, which required minor adjustments but did not affect the overall plan for the warehouse expansion project.
